desc: Ipoh is Malaysia's standout heritage-and-food city: easier on the wallet and calmer than Penang, with dramatic cave temples and fast rail links from Kuala Lumpur.
hls: Signature white-coffee and hawker scene in a compact old town
Limestone cave temples and karst viewpoints minutes from the center
Lower-cost, lower-crowd alternative to Penang for culture + food
Excellent ETS rail stop between Kuala Lumpur and Penang

todo.name: Ipoh Old Town mural + kopitiam walk
todo.desc: Pair Ernest Zacharevic mural lanes with classic white-coffee shops before midday crowds.

todo.name: Kek Lok Tong and Sam Poh Tong caves
todo.desc: Visit both iconic cave temples for shrines, gardens, and limestone chambers.

todo.name: Perak Tong staircase viewpoint
todo.desc: Climb to the hilltop lookout for one of the best city-and-karst panoramas.

todo.name: Concubine Lane at opening hour
todo.desc: Go early to see the heritage shophouse lane before tour-bus bottlenecks.

todo.name: Gua Tempurung guided river cave section
todo.desc: Book the wet section for a less-obvious, high-value half-day adventure outside town.

todo.name: Gunung Lang lakeside stop
todo.desc: Take a quick boat crossing and boardwalk loop for a quieter local park break.

wifi.note: Good fiber in hotels and cafés; mobile data is strong citywide.

prac.fltNote: There are no practical nonstop UK/Ireland-to-Ipoh options: most travelers fly nonstop to Kuala Lumpur (KUL) or sometimes Penang (PEN), then add a domestic hop, ETS train, or 2-3 hour road transfer; Jul-Aug and late-Dec school-holiday windows usually push long-haul fares up first.
prac.currency: Malaysia local currency (check live rates before booking).
prac.lang: Local language plus tourism-level English in main visitor areas.
prac.tz: Check local timezone and DST rules for exact travel dates.
prac.visa: Confirm current entry/transit rules for your passport before booking.
alerts: Afternoon thunderstorms are frequent year-round and can temporarily flood low-lying streets in Old Town.
Popular bean-sprout chicken and noodle shops often sell out before mid-afternoon, especially on weekends.
Ride-hailing works well in town, but return pickups from cave temples can be slower around prayer and school-run times.

sweet: Jan-Mar and Jun-Aug for relatively drier sightseeing windows and easier cave visits.
